1.name: Easy
1.description: Factual questions directly from the text

2.name: Medium
2.description: Interpretive questions about meaning and themes

questions.question: During whose leadership did the events of Ruth begin?

options.A: King David

questions.explanation: Ruth 1:1 says the story begins in the days when the judges ruled.

questions.question: What problem was in the land when Ruth's story began?

options.A: War

questions.explanation: The first verse describes a famine in the land.

questions.question: To which country did Elimelech and his family go to sojourn?

options.A: Egypt

questions.explanation: They went to sojourn in the country of Moab.

questions.question: What was Elimelech’s wife’s name?

options.A: Naomi

questions.explanation: Ruth 1:2 names the wife Naomi.

questions.question: What were the names of Naomi’s two sons?

options.A: Aaron and Eleazar

questions.explanation: The sons were Mahlon and Chilion.

questions.question: Who died first in Naomi’s family while in Moab?

options.A: Elimelech

questions.explanation: Elimelech died and Naomi was left with her two sons.

questions.question: What were the names of the two Moabite women who married Naomi’s sons?

options.A: Esther and Zipporah

questions.explanation: The sons married Orpah and Ruth.

questions.question: What happened to Mahlon and Chilion?

options.A: They returned to Judah

questions.explanation: Both Mahlon and Chilion died, leaving Naomi alone.

questions.question: What news did Naomi hear in Moab that caused her to return home?

options.A: The LORD had visited his people and given them bread

questions.explanation: Naomi heard the LORD had provided bread for His people.

questions.question: What did Naomi tell her daughters-in-law to do when she decided to return to Judah?

options.A: Return each to her mother’s house

questions.explanation: Naomi asked them to return to their mothers’ houses.

questions.question: What did Naomi bless her daughters-in-law with as they parted?

options.A: A long life
options.B: That they may find rest in the house of their husband

questions.explanation: She blessed them to find rest in their husband’s house.

questions.question: What reason did Naomi give for telling them to return to their mother’s house?

options.A: She preferred them to stay in Moab
options.B: She wanted them to remarry quickly
options.C: She was too old to have another husband for them

questions.explanation: Naomi said she was too old to have another husband for them.

questions.question: Which daughter-in-law returned to her people and gods?

options.A: Ruth

questions.explanation: Orpah kissed Naomi and went back to her people and gods.

questions.question: Which young woman said, 'Thy people shall be my people, and thy God my God'?

options.A: Orpah

questions.explanation: Ruth declared she would follow Naomi and her God.

questions.question: What did Ruth promise regarding Naomi's death?

options.A: Where thou diest, will I die, and there will I be buried
options.B: I will leave before you die

questions.explanation: Ruth pledged to die where Naomi died and be buried there.

questions.question: What did the people of Bethlehem say when Naomi returned?

options.A: Is this Ruth?

questions.explanation: They recognized Naomi and asked if it was her.

questions.question: What name did Naomi ask not to be called?

options.A: Naomi

questions.explanation: Naomi asked not to be called Naomi but Mara.

questions.question: What does Naomi say has changed her from being full to empty?

options.A: She lost her son
options.D: The LORD hath brought her home again empty

questions.explanation: Naomi said the LORD brought her home empty.

questions.question: At what harvest season did Naomi and Ruth return to Bethlehem?

options.A: Barley harvest

questions.explanation: They returned in the beginning of the barley harvest.

questions.question: What was Boaz’s relation to Naomi’s husband?

options.A: Brother

questions.explanation: Boaz was a kinsman of Elimelech, Naomi’s husband.

questions.question: What did Ruth ask permission to do in the fields?

options.A: Glean ears of corn after the reapers

questions.explanation: She asked to glean after the reapers.

questions.question: Whose field did Ruth happen to glean in?

options.A: Elimelech’s

questions.explanation: Ruth happened upon Boaz’s field.

questions.question: What blessing did Boaz say to his reapers?

options.A: Good luck to you

questions.explanation: He blessed them with the LORD’s presence.

questions.question: What did Boaz ask his servant about Ruth?

options.A: Whose damsel is this?

questions.explanation: He asked the servant whose young woman she was.

questions.question: How did the servant describe Ruth?

options.A: A Hebrew woman
options.B: The Moabitish damsel that came back with Naomi

questions.explanation: The servant identified her as the Moabitish woman who returned with Naomi.

questions.question: What did Ruth do all day in the field?

options.A: Gleaned after the reapers

questions.explanation: She gleaned throughout the day and stayed until evening.

questions.question: What instruction did Boaz give Ruth regarding other fields?

options.A: Go to another field
options.B: Go not to glean in another field

questions.explanation: He told her to remain in his field.

questions.question: What did Boaz say he had charged his young men not to do?

options.A: Touch her

questions.explanation: He told them not to touch her.

questions.question: How did Ruth respond when Boaz spoke kindly to her?

options.A: She walked away
options.D: She bowed and asked why she found favor

questions.explanation: She bowed and asked why he regarded her kindly.

questions.question: What had Boaz heard about Ruth’s actions toward Naomi?

options.A: All that she had done for Naomi since her husband’s death
options.B: That she had stolen from the field
options.C: That she had left Naomi willingly
options.D: That she had married another man

questions.explanation: Boaz acknowledged all Ruth had done for Naomi.

questions.question: What did Boaz pray the LORD would give Ruth?

options.A: Wealth and riches
options.D: A full reward for her work

questions.explanation: He prayed she would receive a full reward from the LORD of Israel.

questions.question: Why did Ruth say she found favor in Boaz’s sight?

options.A: Because she was wealthy
options.B: Because he comforted her and treated her kindly
options.C: Because she was a king’s daughter

questions.explanation: She said she found favor because he had comforted her.

questions.question: What did Boaz invite Ruth to do at mealtime?

options.A: Eat bread and dip her morsel in vinegar

questions.explanation: He invited her to eat with his reapers.

questions.question: What special provision did Boaz make for Ruth while she gleaned?

options.A: Let some handfuls fall purposely that she might glean them

questions.explanation: He instructed his men to leave extra grain for her to glean.

questions.question: How much did Ruth glean in one day?

options.A: Half an ephah of barley

questions.explanation: She gleaned about an ephah of barley in one day.

questions.question: What did Ruth give Naomi from what she had gleaned?

options.A: What she had reserved after she was satisfied

questions.explanation: Ruth shared the food she had set aside after eating.

questions.question: How did Naomi describe the man Ruth had worked with?

options.A: He is near of kin unto us, one of our next kinsmen
options.B: He is a stranger from Moab

questions.explanation: Naomi recognized Boaz as a relative of Elimelech.

questions.question: What blessing did Naomi pronounce on Boaz?

options.A: May he die with no heir
options.B: Blessed be he of the LORD who hath not left off his kindness

questions.explanation: She blessed Boaz for his kindness toward the living and the dead.

questions.question: Why did Naomi tell Ruth to continue gleaning with Boaz’s maidens?

options.A: So she would not return to Moab
options.D: So she would not be seen in another field

questions.explanation: Naomi wanted Ruth to stay where she was safe and known.

questions.question: What did Naomi ask Ruth to do in order to find rest?

options.A: Return to Moab
options.C: Let her seek rest under a kinsman-redeemer

questions.explanation: Naomi wanted Ruth to seek rest through a kinsman-redeemer.

questions.question: What did Naomi tell Ruth to do before approaching Boaz at the threshing floor?

options.A: Wash, anoint herself, and put on her raiment

questions.explanation: Naomi instructed Ruth to cleanse and dress herself before going to Boaz.

questions.question: Where was Ruth to lie down when Boaz slept?

options.A: On the threshing floor

questions.explanation: Naomi told her to uncover his feet and lie down.

questions.question: What did Ruth do after Boaz had eaten and drunk?

options.A: She left immediately
options.B: She came softly, uncovered his feet, and lay down

questions.explanation: She went quietly to him at his feet after he lay down.

questions.question: What did Ruth ask Boaz to do to her because he was a near kinsman?

options.A: Place a veil on her head

questions.explanation: She asked him to spread his skirt over her as a sign of his protection.

questions.question: How did Boaz describe Ruth in terms of kindness?

options.A: She has shown more kindness in the latter end than at the beginning

questions.explanation: Boaz praised her loyal kindness to Naomi.

questions.question: Why did Boaz say Ruth should not fear?

options.A: Because she would return to Moab
options.B: Because she would become a queen
options.D: Because he would do to her all that she required

questions.explanation: He assured her he would do what she asked if he could.

questions.question: What did Boaz say about the nearer kinsman?

options.A: There is a kinsman nearer than I

questions.explanation: Boaz pointed out a closer relative who has the first right to redeem.

questions.question: What did Ruth carry back to Naomi after visiting Boaz?

options.A: A ring

questions.explanation: Boaz measured out six measures of barley and gave it to her.

questions.question: What did Naomi tell Ruth to do while waiting for the matter to be resolved?

options.A: Sit still until she knows how the matter will fall

questions.explanation: Naomi advised her to wait patiently for the outcome.

questions.question: Where did Boaz sit when he went to speak with the nearer kinsman?

options.A: At the gate

questions.explanation: He sat down at the city gate where business was transacted.

questions.question: How many elders did Boaz summon as witnesses when speaking with the nearer kinsman?

options.A: Five

questions.explanation: He took ten elders with him to serve as witnesses.

questions.question: What did Naomi want to sell to the nearest kinsman?

options.A: A house
options.D: The parcel of land that belonged to Elimelech

questions.explanation: Naomi sought to sell Elimelech’s parcel of land to the kinsman.

questions.question: What condition did Boaz place on buying the field?

options.A: He must marry Naomi
options.B: He must also redeem Ruth the Moabitess to preserve the dead man’s name

questions.explanation: The redemption involved taking Ruth as wife to raise up the dead’s name.

questions.question: Why did the nearer kinsman decline to redeem the land and Ruth?

options.A: Lest he mar his own inheritance
options.D: Because he was not a kinsman

questions.explanation: He declined to avoid damaging his own inheritance.

questions.question: What custom did the kinsman follow to confirm the redemption?

options.A: He wrote a contract
options.B: He paid money in full view
options.C: He plucked off his shoe and gave it to his neighbor

questions.explanation: Taking off the shoe was the customary confirmation of redemption in Israel.

questions.question: What did Boaz say after the nearer kinsman declined?

options.A: Buy it for thyself
options.B: Let it go back to Naomi

questions.explanation: Boaz asked the kinsman to buy the property for himself after he declined.

questions.question: What declaration did Boaz make before the elders and all the people at the gate?

options.A: He would leave the land uncultivated
options.B: That he had bought all that was Elimelech's and also Ruth the Moabitess to be his wife
options.D: That he would build a house of Israel

questions.explanation: He declared the purchase of land and Ruth as his wife to preserve the dead’s name.

questions.question: What reason did Boaz give for taking Ruth as his wife?

options.A: To raise up the name of the dead upon his inheritance

questions.explanation: He sought to preserve Elimelech’s family line through Ruth.

questions.question: How did the elders bless Ruth after Boaz married her?

options.A: That she would return to Moab
options.C: That she would be like Rachel and Leah and bear a famous child in Bethlehem

questions.explanation: They compared her favorably to Rachel and Leah and wished her a famous line in Bethlehem.

questions.question: What was the result of Boaz taking Ruth as his wife?

options.A: The LORD gave her conception and she bore a son

questions.explanation: Ruth gave birth to a son through Boaz.

questions.question: What blessing did the women give Naomi after Ruth bore a son?

options.A: May the LORD forget thee
options.B: Blessed be the LORD who hath not left thee this day without a kinsman

questions.explanation: They praised God for providing Naomi with a kinsman-redeemer.

questions.question: What did the women say Ruth was to Naomi?

options.A: A foreign daughter
options.C: Better to her than seven sons

questions.explanation: They said Ruth was better to Naomi than seven sons.

questions.question: What role did Naomi take with Ruth’s son?

options.A: She took the child and became nurse unto him

questions.explanation: Naomi cared for the child and was his nurse.

questions.question: What name did Naomi’s neighbors give Ruth’s son?

options.A: Boaz

questions.explanation: They named him Obed.

questions.question: What lineage did the women say Obed would belong to?

options.A: He is the father of Jesse, the father of David
options.B: He is the king of Moab

questions.explanation: They announced Obed as ancestor of Jesse and David.

questions.question: What is the significance of Ruth's pledge, 'Thy people shall be my people, and thy God my God'?

options.A: It shows her wholehearted commitment to Naomi and the God of Israel
options.B: It means she wanted land in Bethlehem
options.C: It was a formal marriage vow

questions.explanation: Her statement expresses deep loyalty and faith.

questions.question: How does Ruth’s humility appear in her response to Boaz?

options.A: She refused to eat
options.C: She bowed to the ground and asked why she found favor

questions.explanation: Ruth humbly acknowledged Boaz’s kindness and her own lowly position.

questions.question: Why is Boaz’s blessing of Ruth under the LORD’s wings meaningful?

options.A: It indicates she is a priestess
options.B: It imagery suggests protection, refuge, and divine care
options.C: It refers to an altar only
options.D: It means she will go to the temple

questions.explanation: The blessing evokes God’s protective provision for those who trust Him.

questions.question: Why would Naomi tell Ruth to anoint herself and put on her raiment before going to the threshing floor?

options.A: To prepare for battle
options.B: To present herself respectfully to Boaz and show readiness for marriage

questions.explanation: The preparation signals dignity and intention as she seeks the redeemer.

questions.question: What does Ruth’s request for Boaz to spread his skirt over her symbolize?

options.A: His willingness to take her under his protection and possibly redeem her

questions.explanation: It symbolizes seeking a kinsman redeemer’s protection and care.

questions.question: Why did Boaz mention a nearer kinsman before agreeing to marry Ruth?

options.A: To avoid commitment
options.B: To honor the legal right of the closer relative before claiming his own right
options.D: Because he did not love Ruth

questions.explanation: He respected the established procedure for redemption rights.

questions.question: Why did Boaz give Ruth six measures of barley?

options.A: To pay her wages
options.C: As a gift for Naomi alone
options.D: To show generosity and provide for her mother-in-law and herself

questions.explanation: He provided ample support as a sign of his care.

questions.question: What additional responsibility came with redeeming the land?

options.A: To move to Moab
options.C: To marry Ruth and raise up the dead man’s name

questions.explanation: Redemption included preserving the deceased’s family line through marriage.

questions.question: How did the custom of removing a shoe serve as a testimony?

options.A: It signified shame
options.B: It confirmed a legal transfer of rights in front of witnesses
options.C: It was purely symbolic with no legal meaning
options.D: It was only used in weddings

questions.explanation: The shoe removal made the redemption publicly recognized and binding.

questions.question: What does Boaz’s phrase 'all that was Elimelech's' include?

options.A: His land and his wife by marriage to raise up his name

questions.explanation: It refers to the land and the widow associated with his family name.

questions.question: How did the community’s blessing on Naomi underscore God’s faithfulness?

options.A: They predicted riches
options.C: They praised the LORD for not leaving her without a kinsman

questions.explanation: The blessing recognized God’s provision through Boaz and Ruth.

questions.question: Why did the women say Ruth was better to Naomi than seven sons?

options.A: Because Ruth cared for Naomi in her widowhood with loyal devotion

questions.explanation: Ruth’s loving care surpassed what could be expected even from many sons.

questions.question: What does Obed’s birth signify in the story of Ruth?

options.A: The end of Naomi’s story
options.D: God’s restoration of Naomi and continuation of the family line

questions.explanation: Obed represents redemption and renewed family hope.

questions.question: Which future king is Obed directly related to according to Ruth 4?

options.A: Solomon

questions.explanation: Obed was the father of Jesse, who was the father of David.

questions.question: How does Ruth's declaration reflect the theme of covenant loyalty?

options.A: It shows personal allegiance to Naomi and to the LORD, transcending ethnic boundaries
options.B: It suggests she would remain in Moab
options.C: It is a legal statement only

questions.explanation: Ruth commits herself to Naomi’s people and God, showing deep covenant loyalty.

questions.question: What does Boaz’s protection of Ruth teach about kindness to the marginalized?

options.A: Marginalized people should fend for themselves
options.C: Those in power are called to protect and provide for the vulnerable

questions.explanation: Boaz honors Ruth’s position by ordering her protection and provision.

questions.question: Why is it significant that Boaz invoked 'under whose wings thou art come to trust'?

options.A: It emphasizes military power
options.B: It highlights Ruth’s trust in God and God’s protective care
options.C: It refers to a pagan god

questions.explanation: The imagery shows God as a refuge for those who trust Him.

questions.question: Why did Naomi see Ruth as needing rest?

options.A: Because Ruth was a widow without a husband or inheritance
options.D: Because Ruth wanted to become a priest

questions.explanation: Naomi sought a stable future and security for Ruth.

questions.question: What does Ruth’s obedience to Naomi’s plan reveal about her character?

options.A: She is faithful and humble

questions.explanation: She follows Naomi’s advice faithfully despite the risk.

questions.question: Why did Boaz say Ruth demonstrated more kindness in the latter end than at the beginning?

options.A: Because she had given him gifts
options.B: Because her loyalty and conduct as a widow for Naomi exceeded expectations
options.C: Because she was popular in Bethlehem

questions.explanation: Her continuing devotion after her husband’s death was remarkably loyal.

questions.question: What cultural obligation does Boaz appeal to when speaking to the nearer kinsman?

options.A: The duty to redeem a relative’s property and wife to preserve his name
options.D: The command to build a temple

questions.explanation: He invokes the redeeming duty within the kinsman-redeemer system.

questions.question: What can we infer from the nearer kinsman’s refusal?

options.A: He had no compassion
options.B: He was not a true relative
options.C: He prioritized his own inheritance over family obligation

questions.explanation: He turned down the duty because it might harm his own estate.

questions.question: How does Boaz’s public declaration at the gate affect the redemption?

options.A: It makes the transaction secret
options.D: It provides public legal witness and transparency

questions.explanation: Announcing it before witnesses made the redemption legally binding.

questions.question: What blessing did Ruth receive as a result of her marriage to Boaz?

options.A: She became the mother of a son

questions.explanation: She conceived and bore a son through Boaz.

questions.question: Why is Obed called a comfort to Naomi?

options.A: Because he was a strong warrior
options.B: Because he restored her life and secured her family future

questions.explanation: The birth of Obed brought Naomi comfort and joy after her losses.

questions.question: How does Ruth’s vow reflect a broader message about inclusion in Israel?

options.A: It shows that faith and loyalty can make a foreigner part of God’s people

questions.explanation: Her commitment demonstrates Israel’s openness to those who join its faith and people.

questions.question: What does Naomi’s change of name to Mara signify?

options.A: A celebration of her return
options.B: Her feeling that the LORD had made her life bitter
options.C: Her claim to a royal title

questions.explanation: Naomi identifies with bitterness because of her losses and suffering.

questions.question: What does Ruth asking to glean in the fields reveal about her situation?

options.A: She was wealthy and secure
options.B: She had many relatives to support her
options.C: She was poor, vulnerable, and seeking provision within Israel’s law
options.D: She was looking for a husband first

questions.explanation: Gleaning was a way for poor outsiders to obtain food under Israelite law.

questions.question: Why does Boaz emphasize that Ruth came under the wings of the LORD?

options.A: To show that her trust was ultimately in God, not merely in human kindness
options.D: To say she should leave Bethlehem

questions.explanation: He recognizes her faith and sees God as her protector.

questions.question: How does Boaz’s blessing align with the theme of divine reward in Ruth?

options.A: It removes the need for obedience
options.B: It focuses on material success only
options.C: It expresses belief that God will repay kindness and faithfulness

questions.explanation: The blessing links God’s reward to Ruth’s faithful conduct.

questions.question: Why does Naomi arrange for Ruth to approach Boaz?

options.A: To shame her
options.B: To secure a lawful redeemer and future for Ruth
options.C: To force her into marriage against her will

questions.explanation: Naomi wants Ruth to find security and preservation through redemption law.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'spread therefore thy skirt over thine handmaid' imply about ancient Israelite marriage customs?

options.A: It alludes to a symbolic act of protection and potential marriage by the kinsman-redeemer
options.D: It refers to a harvest ritual

questions.explanation: The request symbolizes seeking familial protection and pledge of marriage.

questions.question: Why does Boaz remind Ruth about the nearer kinsman before he acts?

options.A: To keep her waiting forever
options.D: To follow the legally ordered redemption process

questions.explanation: He wants to ensure all rightful claims are honored under the law.

questions.question: How is the kinsman-redeemer concept illustrated in Ruth?

options.A: Through land redemption coupled with marrying the widow to preserve the deceased’s name

questions.explanation: The redeemer buys the land and marries Ruth to continue the family line.

questions.question: What does the act of removing the shoe reveal about public legal transactions in Israel?

options.A: They were secretive
options.B: They involved symbolic gestures and witnesses to validate contracts

questions.explanation: Such customs made the transfer legally recognized and witnessed.

questions.question: Why is Ruth described as 'the wife of Mahlon' even after his death?

options.A: Because her identity and redemption obligation are tied to Mahlon’s family line
options.B: Because she remained married to Mahlon spiritually

questions.explanation: Her status as Mahlon’s widow is central to the redeeming obligation.

questions.question: How does the mention of Rachel, Leah, and Pharez in the blessing connect Ruth to Israel’s broader history?

options.A: It separates her from Israel’s heritage
options.C: It situates her among foundational mothers and links her offspring to Israel’s covenant lineage
options.D: It suggests she was a queen

questions.explanation: The blessing places Ruth within the continuity of Israel’s family history and promise.

questions.question: What theological theme is underscored by Ruth conceiving through Boaz?

options.A: God’s provision and restoration through covenant faithfulness

questions.explanation: Her conception shows how God restores a family through faithful covenant actions.

questions.question: Why do the women compare Ruth to Rachel and Leah?

options.A: To honor her role in building Israel’s house and continuing the lineage
options.C: To say she would bear many sons
options.D: To claim she was a priestess

questions.explanation: The comparison honors Ruth as a valued ancestress in Israel’s history.

questions.question: What does Obed’s birth teach about the consequences of obedience and loyalty?

options.A: That loyalty brings worldly riches only
options.B: That steadfast faith and kindness can lead to blessing and legacy

questions.explanation: Ruth’s loyalty and Boaz’s faithfulness produced blessing for Naomi and Israel.

questions.question: How does the timing of Ruth’s arrival in Bethlehem at barley harvest set the stage for the story?

options.A: It introduces the agricultural and providential context for her gleaning and meeting Boaz
options.B: It means she would become a farmer
options.C: It indicates she arrived in winter

questions.explanation: The harvest setting is crucial for the plot of gleaning and redemption.

questions.question: Why is Ruth’s decision to glean among the reapers an act of faith?

options.A: Because gleaners were always attacked
options.B: Because she did not know Hebrew
options.C: Because she relied on Israel’s compassionate laws and God’s provision
options.D: Because she wanted to marry quickly

questions.explanation: Her choice trusted that God’s provision laws would protect and provide for her.

questions.question: What cultural insight is revealed by Naomi’s blessing of Boaz?

options.A: Boaz was a foreigner
options.B: Kindness to the living and dead was a treasured virtue in Israelite society

questions.explanation: Naomi highlights Boaz’s steadfast kindness across generations.

questions.question: Why is the night visitation at the threshing floor strategically significant?

options.A: It was meant to deceive Boaz
options.C: It was the only time men ate
options.D: It allowed Ruth to approach Boaz privately while still within social boundaries

questions.explanation: Night provided a respectful opportunity for her request without causing scandal.

questions.question: Why does Boaz say the whole city knows Ruth is a virtuous woman?

options.A: Because her conduct among her neighbors and her loyalty to Naomi were well known
options.C: Because she was from a noble family

questions.explanation: Her reputation was one of virtue and steadfast devotion.

questions.question: What does the involvement of ten elders at the gate indicate about the redemption process?

options.A: It was private
options.B: It required public legal confirmation by community leaders
options.C: It was only a family matter

questions.explanation: Public witnesses were necessary for legitimate legal action.

questions.question: How does the kinsman’s removal of his shoe illustrate the tension between personal interest and family duty?

options.A: He relinquishes his right in favor of Boaz because he chooses self-preservation over the obligation
options.C: He shows he has no legal right

questions.explanation: His act conveys choosing personal inheritance over the duty to redeem.

questions.question: Why is Ruth’s marriage to Boaz framed as both a legal and a relational act?

options.A: Because it was solely a business deal
options.C: Because it involved legal redemption of land and the compassionate duty to care for a widow
options.D: Because it was arranged by Naomi only

questions.explanation: The redemption law blended financial, family, and social obligations.

questions.question: How does the birth of a son to Ruth and Boaz reflect the theme of restoration?

options.A: It restores Naomi’s family line and provides hope after loss
options.B: It fulfills a prophecy from Moab

questions.explanation: The child signals restoration of family and blessing after prior mourning.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'not left thee this day without a kinsman' tell us about God’s role in the story?

options.A: God withdrew from Naomi
options.C: God provided a redeemer in His kindness and care

questions.explanation: The statement acknowledges God’s provision of Boaz as the redeemer.

questions.question: Why is Ruth described as better to Naomi than seven sons?

options.A: Because her personal devotion and care met Naomi’s needs in widowhood

questions.explanation: Ruth’s loyal love exceeded even the strongest family expectation.

questions.question: What do Naomi’s actions with Obed reveal about her restored status?

options.A: She has no influence in the family
options.B: She becomes a respected matriarch and nurse to the new heir

questions.explanation: Naomi assumes an honored role caring for the heir of her household.

questions.question: Why does the genealogy at the end of Ruth matter theologically?

options.A: It connects Ruth and Boaz to King David and Israel’s royal lineage
options.B: It shows that Ruth had no descendants
options.C: It proves Moabites could not marry Israelites

questions.explanation: The genealogy secures Ruth’s place in the ancestry of David and Israel’s covenant history.

questions.question: What does Naomi’s choice to return to Bethlehem express about identity and belonging?

options.A: That she preferred foreign land
options.B: That she longs to return to her people and her God

questions.explanation: Naomi seeks to return to her home community and faith.

questions.question: How does Boaz’s treatment of Ruth reflect Israel’s social law regarding the poor and foreigners?

options.A: He applies the principles of kindness and protection that extend to the marginalized
options.C: He singles her out for punishment
options.D: He claims she has no rights

questions.explanation: His behavior models the care commanded for those in need, including foreigners.

questions.question: What does Ruth’s choice to stay with Naomi teach about covenant loyalty beyond blood relation?

options.A: True loyalty can cross ethnic and cultural boundaries in service to God and family
options.B: Loyalty only applies to blood relatives

questions.explanation: Ruth chooses Naomi’s people and God, showing loyalty is a deliberate covenant decision.

questions.question: Why is the threshing floor an appropriate setting for Ruth’s request to Boaz?

options.A: It is where kings are crowned
options.C: It is a marketplace of foreigners
options.D: It is a place of provision and also where Boaz wheats and prepares grain for his household

questions.explanation: The threshing floor combines agricultural provision with Boaz’s role as provider, making it fitting for the petition.

questions.question: What does Boaz’s reference to the whole city knowing about Ruth suggest about her reputation?

options.A: Her virtue and loyalty were publicly recognized and valued
options.D: She was a stranger no one knew

questions.explanation: Her positive reputation among the people supports Boaz’s favorable treatment.

questions.question: How does the earlier refusal of the nearer kinsman make Boaz’s marriage to Ruth more meaningful?

options.A: It proves Boaz was the only relative
options.B: It shows Boaz voluntarily assumed a duty that another chose to avoid
options.D: It means Ruth was rejected by all

questions.explanation: Boaz’s action highlights his generous commitment to family obligation.

questions.question: What does Boaz mean by buying Ruth to be his wife?

options.A: He is redeeming her as a widow to preserve her deceased husband’s lineage
options.C: He is taking her as a concubine

questions.explanation: The concept relates to redeeming the widow for family preservation under the law.

questions.question: How does the blessing of Boaz and Ruth echo earlier promises to Israel?

options.A: It invokes the continuity of God’s people and God's blessing on their household
options.B: It rejects the promise to Abraham

questions.explanation: The blessing situates their union within God’s ongoing care for Israel.

questions.question: Why does the narrative emphasize that the LORD gave Ruth conception?

options.A: To show the divine source of blessing and the fulfillment of redemption
options.B: To imply human effort was enough

questions.explanation: The mention underscores that God is the author of the blessing.

questions.question: What does it mean that Ruth is better to Naomi than seven sons?

options.A: Ruth is literally seven times better than sons
options.C: Ruth’s devotion provides a deeper kind of blessing than the expected support of multiple sons

questions.explanation: The statement praises Ruth’s unique loyalty and care as surpassing even many sons.

questions.question: How does the birth of Obed fulfill Naomi’s earlier sense of emptiness?

options.A: It makes her decide to go back to Moab
options.B: It replaces her grief with a sense of fullness and future hope

questions.explanation: Obed’s birth brings comfort and restoration to Naomi.

questions.question: What significance does the genealogy have for the wider biblical narrative?

options.A: It traces the ancestral line to David, connecting Ruth’s story to Israel’s monarchy and Messianic line
options.C: It proves Ruth was not a Moabite
options.D: It is unrelated to the story

questions.explanation: The genealogy links Ruth to King David and Israel’s covenant history.

questions.question: What theme emerges from the deaths of Elimelech, Mahlon, and Chilion?

options.A: Loss and the need for divine restoration

questions.explanation: The story begins with tragedy that sets up the need for God’s redeeming provision.

questions.question: Why is Ruth’s declaration often quoted as a model of covenant loyalty?

options.A: Because it demands ethnic purity
options.C: Because it describes a legal contract
options.D: Because it expresses total commitment to Naomi and to the LORD

questions.explanation: It encapsulates Ruth’s selfless devotion and faith choice.

questions.question: What does Ruth's choice of Boaz’s field suggest about divine guidance in the narrative?

options.A: She chose at random
options.C: It suggests providential guidance bringing her to a kind relative
options.D: It was the only field open

questions.explanation: Her arrival in Boaz’s field appears guided by God’s provision.

questions.question: How does Boaz’s response to Ruth illustrate the connection between action and blessing?

options.A: He recognizes her actions of loyalty and rewards them with blessing and provision
options.D: He leaves her to her own devices

questions.explanation: His kindness models how loyal actions lead to blessing.

questions.question: What does Ruth’s nighttime approach to Boaz reveal about her trust in God's plan?

options.A: She seeks to manipulate Boaz
options.B: She humbly follows Naomi’s plan and trusts God through an uncertain situation

questions.explanation: Ruth’s obedience indicates faith and reliance on God’s provision under Naomi’s guidance.

questions.question: What does the nearer kinsman’s right to redeem suggest about ancient property customs?

options.A: Property was sold to the highest bidder
options.B: Land could only be redeemed by priests
options.C: Women could not own any property
options.D: Closer relatives had priority to preserve family inheritance

questions.explanation: The nearest relative had the first right to preserve the family estate.

questions.question: How does the kinsman-redeemer model contribute to Ruth’s inclusion in Israel?

options.A: It legally brings her into the Israelite family through marriage and inheritance
options.B: It keeps her as a foreigner

questions.explanation: The one who redeems her also incorporates her into the family line.

questions.question: Why does Boaz's public declaration before the elders matter for Ruth's status?

options.A: It makes her a slave
options.B: It affirms her legal status as his wife and the legitimacy of the redemption in front of the community

questions.explanation: The public declaration assures both legal and social recognition.

questions.question: In what way does the phrase 'the LORD gave her conception' deepen the story’s meaning?

options.A: It simply notes a medical fact
options.C: It credits God as the source of the blessing and continuation of the family line

questions.explanation: The phrase attributes the blessing of childbirth to God.

questions.question: What does Naomi’s role after Ruth’s marriage show about elder women in Israelite society?

options.A: They had no influence
options.B: They could be central figures of blessing and care in the family

questions.explanation: Naomi becomes a nurturing figure and is honored in the family’s joy.

questions.question: What is the significance of the line 'he is the father of Jesse, the father of David'?

options.A: It links Ruth directly to King David's ancestry
options.B: It proves David was from Moab
options.C: It indicates Obed was a king

questions.explanation: The genealogy establishes Ruth as an ancestor of David.

questions.question: Why might Ruth’s words be considered an example of covenant faithfulness?

options.A: She pledges loyalty to Naomi and to God, not just to family
options.D: She pledges to return to Moab

questions.explanation: Her vow expresses wholehearted faithfulness beyond family duty.

questions.question: How does the metaphor of wings contribute to the understanding of God’s protection?

options.A: It means God is distant
options.B: It refers to a bird sacrifice
options.C: It evokes a picture of God sheltering those who trust Him like a bird shelters its young

questions.explanation: The metaphor communicates intimacy and safety under God’s care.

questions.question: What does Ruth’s return to Naomi with food demonstrate about her priorities?

options.A: That she seeks only her own gain
options.B: That she prioritizes Naomi’s welfare along with her own

questions.explanation: She shares her provision with Naomi, showing mutual care.

questions.question: Why does Boaz mention the city’s knowledge of Ruth as a virtuous woman rather than just his own opinion?

options.A: To affirm her character with the community’s testimony
options.B: To claim he invented her reputation
options.C: To say the city is mistaken

questions.explanation: Community affirmation strengthens the credibility of her virtue.

questions.question: What does the nearer kinsman’s refusal teach about ethical choices in family obligations?

options.A: It is always right to refuse difficult obligations
options.B: Ruth should have chosen another relative
options.C: People may choose self-interest over family duty, but another may step up in righteousness
options.D: Legal rights are more important than mercy

questions.explanation: The narrative contrasts selfish refusal with Boaz’s faithful action.

questions.question: How is the redemption of Ruth also a redemption of Naomi?

options.A: By marrying Ruth, Boaz secures Naomi’s household line and brings her a beloved son
options.D: By sending Naomi back to Moab

questions.explanation: Ruth’s marriage and the birth of Obed restores Naomi’s family and future.

questions.question: What does the prayer for Ruth's house to be like Pharez’s imply about the value of lineage?

options.A: Lineage is unimportant
options.B: It celebrates continuity with Israel’s chosen line and promises honor through descendants

questions.explanation: The prayer affirms the blessing of being part of Israel’s honored heritage.

questions.question: How does the closing genealogy function as a theological statement rather than mere history?

options.A: It shows that Ruth’s faithfulness contributed to the Messianic line leading to David
options.B: It proves Bethlehem was a large city

questions.explanation: The genealogy points to God’s redemptive plan through Ruth and David.

questions.question: What overall message does the book of Ruth communicate through Naomi, Ruth, and Boaz?

options.A: God’s faithful provision and redemption can come through unexpected people and foreign inclusion
options.D: The law always leads to loss

questions.explanation: The story highlights God's redemption for those who trust Him, even through outsiders like Ruth.
